channels.name: Technical documentation and tutorials
channels.audience: Builders who care about privacy, cost, and coding workflows
channels.action: Publish practical implementation guides and runbook-style docs.
channels.output: More active users and stronger trust in the product story.

channels.name: Open-source integrations
channels.audience: Tool maintainers and hands-on engineering teams
channels.action: Ship integration examples with OpenClaw and adjacent tooling.
channels.output: Faster adoption inside tools teams already use.

channels.name: Bay Area meetups, founder dinners, and design-partner sessions
channels.audience: Early design partners and technical decision makers
channels.action: Run local events and targeted design-partner workshops.
channels.output: Higher-quality sales opportunities and more pilot candidates.

channels.name: Strategic partnership outreach
channels.action: Develop joint selling and joint marketing plans.

channels.name: Web3 security and smart-contract audit teams
channels.audience: Security auditors and digital-asset infrastructure teams
channels.action: Publish security-oriented reasoning workflows and benchmarks.

channels.name: Enterprise modernization and reasoning workflows
channels.action: Demonstrate migration planning and controlled reasoning workflows.
channels.output: Higher contract value and more annual contracts.

channels.name: Developer guides by use case
channels.action: Publish repeatable guides for each target use case.
channels.output: Clear education and stronger buying intent.

channels.name: Benchmark and evaluation demos
channels.action: Publish reproducible demos with explicit assumptions.

channels.name: Partner co-marketing
channels.action: Launch joint webinars, posts, and customer stories.
channels.output: Lower-cost awareness and higher inbound quality.

channels.name: Direct outreach to target companies
channels.action: Run focused outreach with role-specific messages.
